name: Ozwyn the Owlbear's Dungeon Escape
description: Ozwyn wakes up in a dungeon with a splitting headache, a bag full of suspicious loot, and several very bad ideas.

hook.title: A Rude Awakening
hook.text: Ozwyn's eyes snap open. Stone ceiling. Iron bars. A bag of clinking loot clutched to his feathered chest. His head throbs like a war drum played by an enthusiastic orc.

choices.label: Inspect the loot bag
choices.hint: What did you steal this time?

critFail.text: The bag is full of live bees. Ozwyn screams, the bees scream, everyone has a terrible time.

fail.text: It's mostly rocks. Decorative rocks, but still rocks. Ozwyn's disappointment is immeasurable.

mixed.text: Some coins, a dagger, and what appears to be someone's lunch. Could be worse.

success.text: Gold coins, a lockpick set, and a very flattering self-portrait of Ozwyn. Past-Ozwyn had taste.

crit.text: The bag contains a small fortune, a map of the dungeon, and a note that says 'You're welcome — Future Ozwyn.' Incredible.

choices.label: Listen at the door
choices.hint: Knowledge is power. Eavesdropping is knowledge.

critFail.text: Ozwyn presses his ear to the door and it swings open, smacking him in the beak. The guards heard that.

fail.text: All Ozwyn hears is his own headache. It's quite loud.

mixed.text: Footsteps. Distant arguing. Something about whose turn it is to feed the prisoners. Useful-ish.

success.text: Guard shift change in five minutes. Left corridor is unpatrolled. Ozwyn's owl-ears earn their keep.

crit.text: Ozwyn overhears the entire guard rotation, the warden's lunch order, and the password to the front gate. His headache was worth it.

choices.label: Eat the suspicious mushroom
choices.hint: It's glowing. That means it's healthy, right?

critFail.text: Ozwyn sees colors that don't exist, speaks fluent Mushroom for eleven minutes, and wakes up in the corner wearing his bag as a hat.

fail.text: It tastes like regret and feet. Ozwyn's headache gets a headache.

mixed.text: Tastes awful but Ozwyn feels slightly more alive. The mushroom glows inside his stomach. That's probably fine.

success.text: The mushroom is actually a rare healing truffle. Ozwyn's headache evaporates and he feels ready to fight a dragon. He won't, but he could.

crit.text: Ozwyn gains temporary night vision, his wounds close, and he can smell treasure through walls. The mushroom winks at him. He winks back.

hook.title: The Note
hook.text: There's a note pinned to Ozwyn's chest. Pinned. With an actual pin. Through his fur. It reads: 'RETURN THE CROWN OR ELSE.' Ozwyn does not remember any crown.

choices.label: Read it carefully
choices.hint: The fine print might save your life.

critFail.text: The note is cursed. The words rearrange into 'FOOL' and the paper dissolves, leaving a rash on Ozwyn's paws.

fail.text: The handwriting is terrible. Ozwyn spends five minutes squinting and learns nothing except that evil people have bad penmanship.

mixed.text: Written by 'Warden Grimjaw.' There's a PS: 'Also you owe me lunch money.' At least Ozwyn knows who's mad at him.

success.text: The back of the note has a partial map scratched in. Someone wanted Ozwyn to escape — or wanted him to think that.

crit.text: Between the lines, a hidden message: 'Third brick from the left. Good luck, handsome.' Past Ozwyn left himself a way out. What a legend.

choices.label: Use it as a napkin
choices.hint: Threats are temporary. Crumbs are annoying.

critFail.text: Ozwyn wipes his beak and the ink stains his face permanently. He now looks like he has a villain mustache. Guards will definitely notice.

fail.text: The note was the only clue to escaping and Ozwyn just blew his nose on it. Classic Ozwyn.

mixed.text: Ozwyn cleans up nicely, feels better about himself, and the note is now useless. Trade-offs.

success.text: As Ozwyn crumples the note, he spots a key taped to the back. Threats come with free lockpicks if you're disrespectful enough.

crit.text: Ozwyn uses the threat letter to clean his face, fold a jaunty hat, and accidentally discovers it's enchanted paper — it now shows the full dungeon layout. Disrespect wins.

choices.label: Fold it into an origami bird
choices.hint: Art knows no bars.

critFail.text: Ozwyn gets a paper cut on his beak that bleeds dramatically. The bird looks like a crumpled sock.

fail.text: It's a terrible bird. More of a wad. Ozwyn is an owlbear, not an artist.

mixed.text: The bird is passable. A nearby rat seems impressed. Ozwyn feels a tiny spark of pride in captivity.

success.text: It's beautiful. Ozwyn throws it through the bars and it glides to a guard who is so moved he drops his keys.

crit.text: The origami bird comes alive, flutters through the bars, steals a key ring, and returns. Ozwyn doesn't question magic anymore.

hook.title: Cellmate
hook.text: A figure stirs in the dark corner of the cell. Someone — or something — else is locked in here with Ozwyn. It groans and sits up.

choices.label: Talk to them
choices.hint: Dungeon friends are still friends.

critFail.text: It's a very angry dwarf who says Ozwyn stole his pants last night. Ozwyn looks down. He is indeed wearing dwarf-sized pants.

fail.text: The cellmate speaks only in riddles and immediately asks Ozwyn a philosophical question. Ozwyn's headache returns with reinforcements.

mixed.text: 'Name's Grub. I've been here three days. The food's terrible but the ambiance is worse.' At least Ozwyn has someone to complain with.

success.text: The cellmate is a retired thief who knows every exit. She draws a map in the dust and says 'You remind me of my idiot son. Go. Be free.'

crit.text: The cellmate is actually the dungeon architect's apprentice, jailed for 'creative differences.' He knows every secret passage, trap, and bathroom. Jackpot.

choices.label: Pretend to be asleep
choices.hint: If you can't see the problem, it can't see you.

critFail.text: Ozwyn fake-snores so loudly the guards come to check. His cellmate throws a rock at him. Everyone is having a bad time.

fail.text: The cellmate pokes Ozwyn repeatedly. 'I know you're awake. Your tail is wagging.' Owlbears don't wag. Or do they?

mixed.text: Ozwyn holds still long enough for the cellmate to monologue about escape plans. Free intel through the power of being annoying.

success.text: The cellmate tiptoes past Ozwyn to escape, leaving behind a lockpick 'for the sleeping one.' Patience pays off.

crit.text: Ozwyn's fake sleep is so convincing the guards declare him dead and open the cell to remove the body. Ozwyn springs up and casually walks out.

choices.label: Power pose to assert dominance
choices.hint: Stand tall. Puff chest. Be the alpha prisoner.

critFail.text: Ozwyn flexes so hard he pulls a muscle and falls over. The cellmate laughs until they cry. Even the rats are snickering.

fail.text: The cellmate is unimpressed. 'I've seen scarier kittens.' Ozwyn's ego takes critical damage.

mixed.text: The cellmate backs up slightly. Not intimidated, exactly, but willing to share the good corner of the cell.

success.text: Ozwyn's pose is so magnificent the cellmate immediately offers to be his sidekick. 'I've never met a real hero before,' they whisper in awe.

crit.text: Ozwyn's power pose radiates such raw energy that the cell bars bend slightly outward. The cellmate kneels. A rat salutes. Legend.

hook.title: The Guard's Lunch
hook.text: A food tray slides under the cell bars. Stale bread, mystery stew, and something that might be cheese or might be a dare. The guard is whistling and not paying attention.

choices.label: Eat the food
choices.hint: Nutrition is important during prison breaks.

critFail.text: The stew is alive. It bites back. Ozwyn loses the fight against his own lunch and retreats to the corner in shame.

fail.text: Tastes like sadness and boot leather. Ozwyn gains nothing but regret and mild nausea.

mixed.text: It's terrible but filling. Ozwyn's stomach stops complaining and starts merely grumbling passive-aggressively.

success.text: Under the bread, a note from a sympathetic cook: 'Side door, midnight, don't tell anyone.' Ozwyn might have a friend out there.

crit.text: The 'cheese' is actually a compressed healing potion. Ozwyn's headache vanishes and he feels like he could benchpress the cell door.

choices.label: Use the tray as a shield
choices.hint: Improvised armor is still armor.

critFail.text: The tray is made of soggy wood. Ozwyn ties it to his arm and it disintegrates immediately. The stew goes everywhere.

fail.text: It's a tray. It blocks exactly nothing important. But Ozwyn feels slightly more prepared, which counts for something.

mixed.text: The tray is dented tin — not great, not terrible. Ozwyn straps it on. He looks ridiculous but marginally safer.

success.text: This is actually a quality tray. Ozwyn fashions a respectable arm-shield. He catches his reflection and looks heroic, in a homeless sort of way.

crit.text: The tray is engraved with a ward symbol. It's enchanted. Ozwyn now has a magic shield that hums when danger is near. Best meal delivery ever.

choices.label: Slide the tray to trip the guard
choices.hint: Physics is the best weapon.

critFail.text: Ozwyn slides the tray directly into the opposite wall. The guard stares. 'Did you just... throw your food at nothing?' Embarrassing.

fail.text: The guard steps over the tray without looking down. Professional. Ozwyn respects it but hates it.

mixed.text: The guard stumbles but catches himself. He shoots Ozwyn a dirty look. Not a full trip, but the seed of chaos has been planted.

success.text: Perfect slide. The guard goes down like a sack of potatoes. His keys skid within reach. Ozwyn snatches them with owl-like precision.

crit.text: The guard slips, does a full backflip, and knocks himself unconscious. His armor, keys, and sandwich are now Ozwyn's. The cellmate applauds.

complication.title: Corridor of Choices
complication.text: Three passages branch from the cell block. One is well-lit with torches. One is pitch dark and moaning. One smells aggressively of cheese.

choices.label: Take the well-lit path
choices.hint: Torches mean civilization. Probably.

critFail.text: The torches are a trap. They explode into smoke and Ozwyn stumbles into a guard barracks mid-poker night. 'Fold,' he says weakly.

fail.text: Well-lit also means well-guarded. Ozwyn walks straight into a checkpoint and has to awkwardly reverse like a spooked cat.

mixed.text: The path is safe but loops around to almost where Ozwyn started. Progress, technically, but only in the philosophical sense.

success.text: The well-lit corridor leads past the kitchens. Ozwyn swipes a pie and a sense of direction. Upward and onward.

crit.text: The torches mark a VIP escape corridor — built for nobles too important to stay imprisoned. Plush carpet, helpful signs, and a complimentary mint.

choices.label: Take the dark path
choices.hint: Fortune favors the blind. Wait, that's not right.

critFail.text: Ozwyn walks face-first into a wall, a spider web, another wall, and something wet. He emerges covered in mystery goo and terror.

fail.text: It's dark. Ozwyn trips, falls, and lies on the ground questioning his life choices for a full minute before getting up.

mixed.text: Ozwyn fumbles through but finds a side passage. It leads somewhere. Where? Who knows. Forward is forward.

success.text: Owl eyes activate. Ozwyn navigates flawlessly and finds a hidden stash of supplies left by previous escapees.

crit.text: The darkness is actually an illusion hiding a secret passage. Ozwyn walks through it like a legend and emerges three floors closer to freedom.

choices.label: Follow the cheese smell
choices.hint: Your nose knows. Probably.

critFail.text: It wasn't cheese. It was a troll's foot spa. Ozwyn has seen things he cannot unsee. The smell will haunt him forever.

fail.text: It's just really old cheese storage. No exits, no treasure, just centuries of mold. Ozwyn's headache approves of none of this.

mixed.text: It leads to the dungeon pantry. No exit, but Ozwyn fills his pockets with cheese wheels. Tactical cheese.

success.text: The cheese smell leads to a supply room with a ventilation shaft big enough for an owlbear. Someone has been smuggling cheese through here.

crit.text: Behind the cheese wall is an entire secret smuggler's tunnel. Cheese was the password all along. Ozwyn grabs supplies and sprints toward freedom, smelling incredible.

complication.title: The Argumentative Lock
complication.text: A heavy oak door blocks the path. Its iron lock has a face — literally. It yawns, notices Ozwyn, and says, 'Oh great, another one. Password?'

choices.label: Pick the lock
choices.hint: It's a lock. With a face. This is fine.

critFail.text: The lock bites Ozwyn's lockpick in half and spits it out. 'Rude! I'm telling the warden!' it shrieks. An alarm echoes.

fail.text: 'That tickles and I hate it,' the lock says. It does not open. Ozwyn's dignity does not open either.

mixed.text: The lock giggles uncomfortably and loosens slightly. 'Fine, ONE turn. But I'm not happy about it.' Ozwyn forces it the rest of the way.

success.text: 'Ooh, you're good,' the lock purrs. It clicks open willingly. 'Come back anytime.' Ozwyn will absolutely not.

crit.text: Ozwyn picks the lock so skillfully it falls in love with him. 'Take me with you,' it whispers. Ozwyn now has a sentient lock as a companion. Sure.

choices.label: Reason with it
choices.hint: Diplomacy works on everything. Even hardware.

critFail.text: 'Your argument is invalid and your fur is unkempt,' the lock snaps. It locks harder out of spite. Ozwyn didn't know locks could do that.

fail.text: The lock listens politely, says 'No,' and goes back to sleep. Ozwyn has been out-negotiated by a piece of metal.

mixed.text: 'Tell me a joke and I'll think about it,' the lock says. Ozwyn tells one. The lock considers. 'Mediocre. But fine.' Click.

success.text: Ozwyn presents a compelling argument about the nature of freedom. The lock weeps a single iron tear and opens. 'Go. Be free. For both of us.'

crit.text: Ozwyn delivers such an eloquent speech that the lock, the door, and the nearby torch are all moved. The door opens itself. The torch bows.

choices.label: Headbutt the door
choices.hint: Owlbear skull vs. oak door. Classic matchup.

critFail.text: Ozwyn's headbutt bounces off the door and he falls backward, seeing stars. The lock laughs so hard it jams permanently. Now no one's getting through.

fail.text: THWACK. The door holds. Ozwyn's headache returns with interest. 'You done?' the lock asks. Ozwyn is not done. Ozwyn should be done.

mixed.text: The door cracks. Not open, but damaged. The lock says 'OW' and Ozwyn feels slightly guilty but mostly proud.

success.text: CRASH. The door splinters, the lock screams 'I QUIT!' and Ozwyn charges through like a feathered battering ram. Beautiful.

crit.text: Ozwyn headbutts the door so hard it explodes into confetti. The lock sails across the hall and embeds in the wall. 'LEGENDARY,' echoes through the dungeon.

complication.title: Goblin Tax Collector
complication.text: A goblin in a tiny suit sits at a makeshift desk in the corridor. A sign reads: 'DUNGEON TOLL — PAY UP OR ELSE.' The goblin adjusts tiny spectacles.

choices.label: Pay the toll
choices.hint: Some fights aren't worth fighting.

critFail.text: The goblin counts the coins, declares them counterfeit, and whistles for backup. Three more goblins in tiny suits appear. Audit time.

fail.text: Ozwyn pays but the goblin demands 'processing fees,' 'corridor maintenance tax,' and 'being-an-owlbear surcharge.' Robbery.

mixed.text: The goblin accepts payment and hands Ozwyn an official receipt. 'Keep this for your records.' Ozwyn does not have records.

success.text: The goblin is so pleased by the exact change that he shares a shortcut. 'Take the left at the skull. VIP detour. Don't tell anyone.'

crit.text: Ozwyn overpays and the goblin is so flustered by the generosity he gives Ozwyn a dungeon loyalty card. 'Tenth escape is free!' What a deal.

choices.label: Negotiate
choices.hint: Surely there's a friends-and-family discount.

critFail.text: Ozwyn's counter-offer offends the goblin's professional pride. 'I studied four years of toll-booth academy for THIS?!' He doubles the price.

fail.text: The goblin sees through every trick. 'Sir, I've dealt with dragons. You're an owlbear in stolen pants. Pay up.'

mixed.text: After fifteen minutes of haggling, Ozwyn gets a 10% discount. The goblin looks defeated. Ozwyn feels oddly guilty.

success.text: Ozwyn talks the toll down to a joke and a handshake. The goblin cackles, stamps a pass, and waves him through. 'First owlbear to make me laugh.'

crit.text: Ozwyn negotiates so well the goblin ends up paying HIM for the privilege of meeting a 'real adventurer.' The goblin also offers to be his accountant.

choices.label: Challenge to a staring contest
choices.hint: Owlbears don't blink. Biological advantage.

critFail.text: Ozwyn blinks first. The goblin erupts in victory, dances on his desk, and charges triple. Ozwyn's pride and wallet are both empty.

fail.text: The goblin has terrifying staring endurance. After three minutes, Ozwyn's eyes water and he concedes. The toll stays.

mixed.text: It's a draw. Both parties are impressed. The goblin cuts the toll in half as a sign of mutual respect. 'You've got good eyes, bird-bear.'

success.text: Twenty seconds in, the goblin cracks. 'STOP. You win. Your eyes are terrifying. Go. PLEASE go.' Ozwyn passes toll-free.

crit.text: Ozwyn's owl-stare is so intense the goblin has a spiritual awakening, quits his job on the spot, and gives Ozwyn his desk drawer of confiscated loot.

complication.title: Suspicious Puddle
complication.text: A puddle sits perfectly centered in the corridor. It ripples despite no wind. It's too clean. It's too round. It's watching Ozwyn.

choices.label: Walk around it very carefully
choices.hint: Never trust standing water in a dungeon.

critFail.text: The puddle takes offense to being avoided and lunges sideways. Ozwyn is now soaking wet and the puddle is snickering. Puddles can't snicker. This one does.

fail.text: Ozwyn carefully circles the puddle and steps into a DIFFERENT, non-sentient puddle. Wet socks. The worst dungeon hazard.

mixed.text: Safe passage. The puddle burbles sadly as Ozwyn goes by. Is he... is he feeling guilty about a puddle?

success.text: As Ozwyn skirts the edge, he spots a drain cover the puddle was guarding. Beneath it: a shortcut. Sorry, puddle.

crit.text: The puddle is so impressed by Ozwyn's caution it parts like a tiny sea, revealing treasure underneath. 'A wise one,' it gurgles. 'Rare down here.'

choices.label: Jump over it heroically
choices.hint: Air superiority.

critFail.text: Ozwyn leaps, the puddle rises like a geyser, and he lands face-first on the other side, completely drenched. The puddle does a victory ripple.

fail.text: Ozwyn's jump is short. One foot in the puddle. It's warm. It shouldn't be warm. Bad. Bad bad bad.

mixed.text: Clean jump! The puddle splashes indignantly but Ozwyn is already on the other side. Small victories.

success.text: Ozwyn sails over with athletic grace he didn't know he had. The puddle makes an impressed 'bloop' noise. Style points.

crit.text: Ozwyn does a triple-flip over the puddle, sticks the landing, and the puddle applauds with three tiny waves. A nearby skeleton gives a thumbs-up.

choices.label: Drink from it
choices.hint: Hydration is hydration.

critFail.text: It tastes like memories, regret, and someone else's Tuesday. Ozwyn is now cursed to occasionally hear puddle thoughts. They're boring.

fail.text: Ozwyn sips and the puddle sips back. He doesn't know what that means and doesn't want to.

mixed.text: It's... actually not bad? Tastes like spring water with a hint of existential dread. Refreshing?

success.text: The puddle is a minor water elemental. Grateful for the attention, it shares dungeon secrets via ripple-patterns. Good puddle.

crit.text: Ozwyn drinks the entire puddle and absorbs its power. He can now sense water sources, hidden passages, and the emotional state of liquids. Incredible and useless.

complication.title: The Alarm
complication.text: CLICK. Ozwyn looks down. His big owlbear foot is on a pressure plate. A bell begins to ring somewhere above. That can't be good.

choices.label: Hide immediately
choices.hint: If they can't find you, the alarm is just a bell.

critFail.text: Ozwyn hides behind a pillar the width of a stick. His entire body is visible. A guard walks by, makes eye contact, and sighs.

fail.text: Ozwyn finds a barrel but can't fit. He's stuck half-in, half-out when the guards arrive. 'Aren't you the escapee?' 'I'm a barrel.'

mixed.text: Ozwyn squeezes into an alcove. Guards run past. One pauses, sniffs, shrugs, and moves on. Too close.

success.text: Ozwyn finds a perfect hiding spot behind a tapestry. Guards search for ten minutes and give up. 'Probably a rat on the plate again.'

crit.text: Ozwyn's hiding spot has a secret door behind it, leading to a guard-free shortcut. The alarm actually helped. Life is beautiful.

choices.label: Act casual
choices.hint: Confidence is the best disguise.

critFail.text: Ozwyn leans against the wall, crosses his arms, and says 'Sup' to the arriving guards. They do not think this is casual. They have swords.

fail.text: Ozwyn whistles and walks slowly. The guards are suspicious but can't technically prove he's not supposed to be here. 'Papers?' Ozwyn doesn't have papers.

mixed.text: 'Just doing my rounds,' Ozwyn says with a clipboard he found. The guards are confused enough to hesitate. Ozwyn walks briskly away.

success.text: Ozwyn acts so naturally the guards assume he's maintenance. 'That alarm goes off every Tuesday,' he says. They nod and leave.

crit.text: Ozwyn convinces the guards that HE triggered the alarm to test THEIR response time. He grades them a C-minus and they apologize. Power.

choices.label: Run for your life
choices.hint: Cardio doesn't care about stealth.

critFail.text: Ozwyn sprints directly into a dead end. He turns around. The guards are already there. 'Nice run though,' one says.

fail.text: Ozwyn runs but his loot bag jingles with every step, creating a perfect audio trail. Stealth was never an option.

mixed.text: Ozwyn outruns two guards but the third is annoyingly athletic. He barely loses them around a corner, panting and sweaty.

success.text: Owlbears are surprisingly fast. Ozwyn reaches top speed and the guards give up after one corridor. 'Not paid enough for this.'

crit.text: Ozwyn runs so fast he breaks the sound barrier — for a dungeon. He leaves a trail of scattered papers and stunned guards. By the time anyone reacts, he's floors away.

complication.title: Rival Escapee
complication.text: Another prisoner rounds the corner at full sprint — a kobold in shackles, eyes wild with freedom-lust. They skid to a halt at the sight of Ozwyn. An awkward pause.

choices.label: Team up
choices.hint: Two escapees are better than one.

critFail.text: The kobold agrees enthusiastically, then immediately triggers every alarm in the hallway. 'Oops! First escape!' Ozwyn has made a terrible ally.

fail.text: They team up but can't agree on direction. After five minutes of arguing at a crossroads, both are hopelessly lost.

mixed.text: The kobold knows one useful shortcut. Ozwyn provides the muscle. Together they are... slightly more competent than alone.

success.text: The kobold is a demolitions expert. Within minutes, they've cleared two blocked passages and befriended a spider. Dream team.

crit.text: The kobold turns out to be a former dungeon engineer. Together, they navigate like they built the place. 'You're the best cellmate I never had,' the kobold sniffles.

choices.label: Race them to the exit
choices.hint: First one out wins bragging rights forever.

critFail.text: Ozwyn sprints, trips over the kobold's chain, and they both tumble down a staircase into a basement. Negative progress.

fail.text: The kobold is shockingly fast. Ozwyn eats dust. Literal dust. The dungeon is very dusty.

mixed.text: Dead heat. Both arrive at the next junction gasping. The kobold wheezes, 'Tie?' Ozwyn wheezes back, 'Tie.' Mutual respect.

success.text: Ozwyn's bigger legs win the day. He reaches the junction first and takes the better path. The kobold yells 'REMATCH!' from behind.

crit.text: Ozwyn outruns the kobold so decisively that other prisoners stop to watch. Someone cheers. The kobold bows in defeat. A dungeon sports legend is born.

choices.label: Trip them as a distraction
choices.hint: You don't have to outrun the guards...

critFail.text: Ozwyn sticks out his foot and trips himself. The kobold hops over, waves, and disappears. Guards find Ozwyn on the floor.

fail.text: The kobold dodges the trip and gives Ozwyn a look of pure betrayal. 'I THOUGHT WE WERE COOL.' They weren't. But still.

mixed.text: The kobold stumbles and the guards chase them instead. Ozwyn feels bad for exactly two seconds before using the distraction to advance.

success.text: Perfect trip. The kobold creates enough chaos to occupy every guard on the floor. Ozwyn sneaks past like a shadow. A mean, owlbear-shaped shadow.

crit.text: The kobold trips so spectacularly they accidentally take out three guards in a domino effect. Ozwyn walks through the carnage untouched. 'Nothing personal,' he whispers.

opportunity.title: Treasure Alcove
opportunity.text: Behind a loose brick, Ozwyn finds a hidden alcove stuffed with glittering trinkets, coins, and one very pretty tiara. Previous escapees' stash?

choices.label: Take everything
choices.hint: Greed is just ambition with pockets.

critFail.text: The alcove is booby-trapped. Ozwyn grabs the tiara and a net drops on him. He's now a jewel-encrusted burrito.

fail.text: There's too much loot and it all falls out of Ozwyn's bag, making a noise like a chandelier crashing. Subtle.

mixed.text: Ozwyn stuffs his bag until it's bursting. He can barely walk but he's RICH. Priorities.

success.text: Everything fits perfectly. Ozwyn's bag is heavy with treasure and he's practically skipping. Crime pays. Escape crime pays double.

crit.text: Among the treasure is a Ring of Feather Fall. Ozwyn takes everything and the ring activates, making his heavy bag weightless. Greedy and graceful.

choices.label: Take only what fits
choices.hint: Restraint is the mark of a professional.

critFail.text: Ozwyn carefully selects items and picks the one cursed ring. It won't come off and it hums a tiny annoying song constantly.

fail.text: Ozwyn picks coins, but they're all dungeon-mint — worthless outside. He carries dead weight.

mixed.text: A sensible haul. Some coins, a dagger, nothing cursed. Ozwyn feels responsible and slightly bored by it.

success.text: Ozwyn picks the most valuable items with expert precision. Maximum value, minimum weight. He was born for heists.

crit.text: Ozwyn selects a small amulet that turns out to be the most valuable item — a Ward of Safe Passage. It hums protectively. Quality over quantity.

choices.label: Leave a thank-you note
choices.hint: Manners cost nothing.

critFail.text: Ozwyn writes 'Ozwyn was here' in full name. The guards now have his identity and handwriting. Great escaping, detective.

fail.text: The note is too polite. Future prisoners will think a very courteous ghost lives in the wall.

mixed.text: Ozwyn writes encouragement for future escapees. It's oddly touching. He takes nothing but leaves something behind.

success.text: 'Good luck — Ozwyn.' The note becomes dungeon legend. Future prisoners will speak of the generous owlbear who paid it forward.

crit.text: Ozwyn's note is so inspiring it starts a prison revolution three weeks later. Guards will blame 'that bloody owlbear' for years. The legend grows.

opportunity.title: Friendly Rat
opportunity.text: A brown rat with a knowing look sits at a crossroads, cleaning its whiskers. It makes eye contact with Ozwyn and nods toward the left tunnel. Smart rat.

choices.label: Follow the rat
choices.hint: Local knowledge is invaluable.

critFail.text: The rat leads Ozwyn directly into a snake nest. Was the rat working for the snakes? Is this a rat-snake alliance? Betrayal.

fail.text: The rat runs too fast and Ozwyn loses it at a junction. He's now lost AND ratless. The worst combination.

mixed.text: The rat leads Ozwyn through a safe corridor but then disappears into a crack. Useful while it lasted.

success.text: The rat navigates Ozwyn past two guard patrols and a trap. At the end, it squeaks proudly. Ozwyn has never respected a rodent more.

crit.text: The rat leads Ozwyn to a hidden exit, waits at the threshold, and salutes with one tiny paw. Ozwyn salutes back, tears in his owl-eyes. Best friend.

choices.label: Offer it supplies
choices.hint: The way to a guide's heart is through their stomach.

critFail.text: Ozwyn offers cheese. The rat is lactose intolerant and takes extreme offense. It squeaks a war cry and bites Ozwyn's toe.

fail.text: The rat sniffs the offering, turns its nose up, and leaves. Ozwyn has been judged by a rat and found wanting.

mixed.text: The rat eats, nods approvingly, and squeaks twice. It might mean 'go right.' Ozwyn goes right. It seems correct-ish.

success.text: The rat accepts the gift and brings back three friends. They form a tiny honor guard and lead Ozwyn through the dungeon. Rat VIP treatment.

crit.text: The rat summons an army of dungeon rats who create a living carpet of safe passage. Ozwyn walks over traps, past guards, through locked doors. The Rat King endorses him.

choices.label: Name it and adopt it
choices.hint: Every hero needs a sidekick.

critFail.text: Ozwyn names it 'Sir Biscuit' and it immediately runs away. Even rats don't want to be called that.

fail.text: The rat tolerates being named 'Crumbles' but refuses to ride on Ozwyn's shoulder. Some boundaries are sacred.

mixed.text: 'Sir Nibbles' seems happy with the name. He climbs onto Ozwyn's shoulder and sometimes points at things. Mostly cheese.

success.text: 'Captain Whiskers' joins permanently. He scouts ahead, squeaks at danger, and looks adorable. Ozwyn's reputation as a beast-whisperer grows.

crit.text: Ozwyn names it 'Duke Rattington III' and it immediately demonstrates it can unlock doors, disarm traps, and do a backflip. Best recruitment decision ever.

opportunity.title: Armory of Rejects
opportunity.text: A storage room full of confiscated gear. Most of it is junk, but one owlbear's junk is another owlbear's arsenal. Time to shop.

choices.label: Grab a weapon
choices.hint: Violence isn't the answer, but it's a strong argument.

critFail.text: Ozwyn grabs a sword that immediately breaks. The blade flies across the room and cuts a rope holding up shelves. Avalanche of junk.

fail.text: The only weapon that fits Ozwyn's grip is a soup ladle. Technically threatening. Technically.

mixed.text: A rusty but serviceable mace. It'll do. Ozwyn gives it an experimental swing and only breaks one shelf.

success.text: Hidden under a pile of broken shields, a perfectly balanced short sword with 'Return to Ozwyn' engraved on it. PAST OZWYN PLANNED FOR THIS.

crit.text: Ozwyn finds a flaming sword. It's literally on fire. It shouldn't be. He takes it. It sings a war hymn. This is the best day of his life.

choices.label: Take the ridiculous hat
choices.hint: Fashion is the ultimate weapon.

critFail.text: The hat is cursed. It won't come off and makes Ozwyn compulsively bow to everyone, including enemies. Horrifying.

fail.text: It's a jester's hat with bells. Every step jingles. Stealth is now impossible. Ozwyn looks spectacular though.

mixed.text: A wide-brimmed hat with a feather. It doesn't do anything useful but Ozwyn feels 40% more confident. Placebo armor.

success.text: The hat is a guard captain's helmet. Ozwyn puts it on and suddenly looks official. Guards salute him. This is the power of hats.

crit.text: The hat is a Crown of Minor Illusion. While wearing it, Ozwyn can look like anyone. He chooses 'guard captain' and strolls through the dungeon like he owns it. He might, soon.

choices.label: Set booby traps behind you
choices.hint: The best defense is a creative offense.

critFail.text: Ozwyn sets a trap and immediately walks into it himself. He hangs upside-down from the ceiling for three embarrassing minutes.

fail.text: The traps are too obvious. Guards step around them easily. 'Amateur,' one mutters. Ozwyn is offended.

mixed.text: One trap works, one doesn't. A guard trips but his partner helps him up. 50% success rate isn't bad for an owlbear.

success.text: Ozwyn rigs three hallways with confiscated gear. Crashes, shouts, and creative cursing echo behind him. Nobody's following.

crit.text: Ozwyn creates a Rube Goldberg machine of dungeon chaos. A cascade of falling shelves, tripwires, and one angry helmet creates a blockade that'll take hours to clear.

opportunity.title: The Merchant Ghost
opportunity.text: A translucent figure floats behind a spectral counter. 'WELCOME TO GARY'S GHOST GOODS,' reads the ectoplasmic sign. 'Competitive prices. No refunds. I'm dead.'

choices.label: Buy something
choices.hint: Ghost economics are... unique.

critFail.text: Ozwyn buys a 'health potion' that's just ghost tears in a bottle. He drinks it and can see through walls — his own hands. Temporarily incorporeal. Terrifying.

fail.text: Everything is overpriced. Ozwyn buys a 'dungeon map' that's just Gary's old grocery list. 'All sales final,' Gary says smugly.

mixed.text: Ozwyn buys a flickering ghost lantern. It lights up most of the time. Ghost-quality goods are exactly as advertised.

success.text: Gary sells Ozwyn a skeleton key (literally carved from a skeleton). It works on basic locks and Gary whispers escape tips. Customer service isn't dead.

crit.text: Gary has a 'going-out-of-afterlife sale.' Ozwyn gets a pouch of shadow-coins (accepted by dungeon merchants), a health draught, and a hug. Gary needed the hug.

choices.label: Haggle with a ghost
choices.hint: What's he going to do, haunt you harder?

critFail.text: Gary is a ghost with nothing to lose. He lowers prices to zero and gives Ozwyn cursed items out of spite. 'Free! ALL CURSED! ENJOY!'

fail.text: 'I've been dead for 200 years. You think prices SCARE me?' Gary is unmoved. Living customer negotiation immunity.

mixed.text: Gary caves slightly. 'Fine. Friends-and-family discount. Even though you're neither.' A small win.

success.text: Ozwyn's sob story about waking up in prison gets Gary emotional. Ghosts can cry? They can. Massive discount and heartfelt advice.

crit.text: Gary is so charmed he offers to possess a guard and walk Ozwyn right out the front entrance. 'I haven't had a body in years. This'll be FUN.'

choices.label: Ask for directions
choices.hint: He's been here forever. Literally.

critFail.text: Gary gives directions to his favorite haunt — a dead end with his tombstone. 'Oh wait, you wanted OUT? Should've said so.' Ghost humor.

fail.text: Gary's directions are for a dungeon layout from 200 years ago. Things have changed. 'The lava room is new?!' Ozwyn screams.

mixed.text: Gary gives somewhat accurate directions filtered through ghost-brain. 'Left, left, through the wall — oh wait, you can't do that — right instead.'

success.text: Gary's been watching prisoners escape for centuries. He knows the three best routes and which guards are lazy. Invaluable intel.

crit.text: Gary reveals a ghost-only passage that living creatures can use 'if they hold their breath and think sad thoughts.' Ozwyn does both. It works. Who knew.

opportunity.title: Healing Fountain?
opportunity.text: A stone basin glows with soft blue light. The water sparkles invitingly. A sign reads: 'DRINK AT OWN RISK. Management not liable for tentacles.'

choices.label: Drink from it
choices.hint: Blue glowing water has never been bad. Right?

critFail.text: Tentacles. The sign was not joking. Ozwyn fights off a fountain-squid and emerges wetter, angrier, and somehow more wounded.

fail.text: The water tastes like pool water and disappointment. Ozwyn's wounds unchanged, his faith in dungeon plumbing destroyed.

mixed.text: Minor healing. The headache dulls from 'war drum' to 'gentle bongo.' Progress.

success.text: The water is genuinely magical. Ozwyn's wounds knit, his headache fades, and he feels ten years younger. What a fountain.

crit.text: Ozwyn drinks and the fountain blesses him with full restoration and a faint blue glow. He's healed, powered up, and looks absolutely majestic.

choices.label: Wash the headache away
choices.hint: A cold splash fixes most things.

critFail.text: The water is not cold. It's sentient. It crawls up Ozwyn's face and tries to become a hat. WATER HAT IS NOT HEALING.

fail.text: Ozwyn splashes his face. It's refreshing but his headache just moves to the other side of his skull. Migrated migraine.

mixed.text: The cool water helps. Headache drops from 'unbearable' to 'merely horrible.' Ozwyn will take it.

success.text: The water soothes everything it touches. Ozwyn's headache melts away and his bruises fade. He almost feels good.

crit.text: The fountain recognizes a worthy soul. It heals Ozwyn completely and whispers the location of every trap ahead. Best spa day ever.

choices.label: Bottle some for later
choices.hint: Portable healing? Don't mind if I do.

critFail.text: The fountain water evaporates the instant it leaves the basin. Ozwyn is left holding an empty bottle and a grudge against physics.

fail.text: Ozwyn bottles the water but it immediately goes flat. Healing soda without the fizz. Useless.

mixed.text: The water retains some glow in the bottle. It'll probably work once. Maybe. Dungeon healthcare is unreliable.

success.text: Two full bottles of glowing healing water. Ozwyn now has portable medicine and the smuggest expression an owlbear can make.

crit.text: The water concentrates in the bottle, becoming a potent healing elixir. It also fizzes pleasantly. Ozwyn has crafted a legendary potion using only luck and a stolen bottle.

opportunity.title: Dungeon Graffiti
opportunity.text: The walls are covered in scratched messages from past prisoners. 'Turn left at the skull,' 'DON'T trust the cheese,' and 'Gronk was here (sorry about the smell).'

choices.label: Follow the scratched advice
choices.hint: Crowdsourced wisdom.

critFail.text: Ozwyn follows 'GO DOWN FOR FREEDOM' and descends three floors deeper into the dungeon. The graffiti artist was either a liar or very confused.

fail.text: Contradictory advice. 'Go left' next to 'NEVER go left.' Ozwyn goes left. It's a broom closet. Neither graffiti was right.

mixed.text: 'Avoid the third corridor' saves Ozwyn from a patrol. He mentally thanks whatever prisoner was helpful between the curse words.

success.text: The messages paint a clear escape route when read in order. Previous prisoners collectively mapped the dungeon through graffiti. Collaborative brilliance.

crit.text: One message is in owlbear claw-marks: 'Ozwyn, window in the east tower. I knew you'd be back. — Ozwyn.' He's escaped before and left himself notes. LEGEND.

choices.label: Add your own message
choices.hint: Leave your mark. Literally.

critFail.text: Ozwyn scratches 'OZWYN WAS HE—' and a guard rounds the corner. 'So YOU'RE Ozwyn. Thanks for the confirmation.' Oops.

fail.text: Ozwyn writes an inspirational quote but misspells three words. Future prisoners will be confused and slightly judgy.

mixed.text: 'Don't give up — Ozwyn' scratched into the wall. It takes time but Ozwyn feels better having done it. Emotional investment in dungeon infrastructure.

success.text: Ozwyn adds useful directions, guard schedules, and a tiny self-portrait. This wall just became the most valuable resource in the dungeon.

crit.text: Ozwyn scratches a complete survival guide and signs it with a dashing self-portrait. Within weeks, this wall will be a shrine. The Ozwyn legacy begins.

choices.label: Study the scratched map
choices.hint: Somewhere in this mess is actual cartography.

critFail.text: Ozwyn stares at the map so long he doesn't notice a patrol approaching. 'Interesting wall, huh?' says a guard. Oops.

fail.text: The map was drawn by someone who clearly never escaped. It ends with a drawing of a skeleton giving a thumbs-up. Ominous.

mixed.text: Parts of the map are helpful. Ozwyn memorizes what he can — trap locations, guard rooms, and someone's doodle of a cat.

success.text: The map is remarkably accurate. Ozwyn traces a clear path to the surface, mentally bookmarking three alternate routes.

crit.text: The map reveals a secret passage marked with an owlbear claw symbol. Someone designed an escape route specifically for owlbear-kind. Ozwyn sheds a proud tear.

crisis.title: The Boss Room
crisis.text: A massive chamber. A stone golem stands in the center, eyes flickering to life. The exit is behind it. Because of course it is.

choices.label: Fight it head-on
choices.hint: Owlbear vs. golem. A tale as old as dungeons.

critFail.text: The golem flicks Ozwyn across the room like a chess piece. He embeds in the wall. Cartoon-style. His pride is more damaged than his body. Barely.

fail.text: Ozwyn lands a heroic punch. The golem doesn't notice. It's made of stone. Ozwyn's fist is not.

mixed.text: A grueling fight. Ozwyn chips the golem's arm off but takes serious hits. Pyrrhic victory at best. The exit is open, at least.

success.text: Ozwyn finds the golem's weak spot and UNLOADS. Rocks fly, the golem crumbles, and Ozwyn stands victorious atop the rubble. HEROIC.

crit.text: Ozwyn screams a battle cry so fearsome the golem's magic core cracks from vibration alone. It crumbles. Ozwyn walks through the dust like an action hero. LEGENDARY.

choices.label: Sneak past while it wakes up
choices.hint: Golems are slow, right? RIGHT?

critFail.text: Ozwyn sneezes mid-sneak. The golem tracks the sound. Owlbear tiptoeing is not as quiet as Ozwyn believed.

fail.text: Almost there — Ozwyn steps on a loose stone. The golem's head rotates 180 degrees. Nightmare fuel.

mixed.text: The golem spots Ozwyn but is too slow to react. Ozwyn scrambles past, scraping the exit doorframe. Close enough.

success.text: Owl-soft footsteps. The golem never fully activates. Ozwyn is through the door before its eyes even glow. Stealth master.

crit.text: Ozwyn moves like a ghost. He passes the golem, pauses to tie its shoelaces together (it has sandals??), and prances through the exit. Style AND substance.

choices.label: Bribe it with loot
choices.hint: Everyone has a price. Even rocks.

critFail.text: Ozwyn offers gold. The golem eats the coins, grows 10% larger, and looks hungrier. That was a feeding, not a bribe. Oh no.

fail.text: The golem stares blankly at the offered treasure. It's a rock. Rocks don't shop. Ozwyn feels foolish.

mixed.text: The golem considers the shiny coins, distracted just long enough for Ozwyn to dash past. Not a bribe — a diversion.

success.text: Ozwyn places gold at the golem's feet. Its programming conflicts — destroy intruder vs. protect treasure. It freezes. Ozwyn walks past a buffering golem.

crit.text: The tiara from the treasure alcove fits perfectly on the golem's head. It looks in a reflection, likes what it sees, and waves Ozwyn through. Fashionable diplomacy.

crisis.title: Collapsing Tunnel
crisis.text: CRACK. The ceiling groans. Dust rains. Stones fall. The tunnel ahead is collapsing in slow motion. Freedom is on the other side. So is death.

choices.label: Sprint through
choices.hint: Speed versus gravity. Classic.

critFail.text: A boulder clips Ozwyn's tail and he goes down. He crawls out, alive but thoroughly flattened in spirit (and slightly in body).

fail.text: Ozwyn makes it through but gets hit by falling debris. He's out but his everything hurts.

mixed.text: A dive-roll through the last gap. Ozwyn emerges dusty, scratched, and alive. Not graceful, but effective.

success.text: Ozwyn runs like the wind and clears the collapse with seconds to spare. Rocks crash behind him like applause.

crit.text: Ozwyn sprints, slides, and dolphin-dives through a closing gap so perfectly it looks choreographed. The tunnel seals behind him. No one's following. CINEMATIC.

choices.label: Brace the support beams
choices.hint: Engineering under pressure. Literally.

critFail.text: Ozwyn grabs a beam and it snaps. The ceiling accelerates its collapse. He's made it worse. Much worse. He runs anyway.

fail.text: Ozwyn holds a beam but it's too heavy. He manages to slow the collapse just enough to scramble under. His shoulders will never forgive him.

mixed.text: Bracing works temporarily. Ozwyn holds the ceiling long enough to squeeze through. His bear-strength was earned today.

success.text: Ozwyn holds the tunnel open with raw strength while debris bounces off his back. Others escape behind him. He's the last one through and he's a HERO.

crit.text: Ozwyn bears the weight of the entire tunnel section single-handedly (single-pawedly?). The stone bends to his will. He steps through as the tunnel seals, untouched. ICON.

choices.label: Accept your fate dramatically
choices.hint: If you're going out, go out with flair.

critFail.text: Ozwyn gives a dramatic speech to no one, lies down, and a rock lands on his tail. He's fine but his speech? Wasted on rubble.

fail.text: Ozwyn closes his eyes, embraces destiny — and the collapse stops. Just crumbling. So his dramatic acceptance was premature. Awkward.

mixed.text: Ozwyn's dramatic pose is so absurd the universe takes pity. A beam falls perfectly to create a shelter. Sometimes style IS substance.

success.text: Ozwyn shouts 'NOT TODAY!' and the tunnel collapse... pauses? As if the dungeon is reconsidering? He sprints through the hesitation. Willpower!

crit.text: Ozwyn shouts 'THE DUNGEON WILL NOT CLAIM ME!' with such conviction the rocks rearrange into a safe archway. Main character energy is REAL.

crisis.title: Guard Patrol
crisis.text: Six guards stand between Ozwyn and the exit gate. They're armed, armored, and one is eating a sandwich. The exit is RIGHT THERE.

choices.label: Disguise yourself as a guard
choices.hint: If you can't beat them, become them.

critFail.text: Ozwyn's disguise is a helmet on top of his owl head. He looks like a seven-foot bird in a too-small hat. 'That's the prisoner.' 'Yep.'

fail.text: The disguise almost works until a guard asks for Ozwyn's badge number. 'Uh... seven?' Wrong answer.

mixed.text: Two guards buy it. Four don't. Ozwyn makes it halfway before someone says, 'Why does that guard have feathers?' Sprint time.

success.text: 'New transfer from the east wing,' Ozwyn grunts. The guards nod. He walks through the checkpoint with practiced authority. Act like you belong.

crit.text: Ozwyn's disguise is perfect. He not only passes through but is handed a shift roster. 'You're on break, new guy.' He walks out the FRONT DOOR.

choices.label: Create a distraction
choices.hint: Look over there!

critFail.text: Ozwyn throws a rock as a distraction. It bounces off a wall and hits him in the back of the head. The guards watch this happen.

fail.text: Ozwyn yells 'FIRE!' Two guards check. Four don't. Still too many.

mixed.text: Ozwyn rolls a barrel down the corridor. Most guards investigate. One stays. Better odds, at least.

success.text: Ozwyn triggers a distant alarm with a thrown coin. All six guards sprint toward it. Empty exit. Beautiful.

crit.text: Ozwyn starts a small, contained fire in a trash pile. Panic. Guards evacuate prisoners. In the chaos, Ozwyn walks out as a 'concerned citizen.' Oscar-worthy.

choices.label: Walk up and say 'I'm the inspector'
choices.hint: Maximum confidence, minimum planning.

critFail.text: 'The inspector is a halfling named Greg.' 'I'm... Greg's assistant?' 'Greg doesn't have an assistant.' Swords drawn.

fail.text: The guards laugh. Heartily. One wipes a tear. 'Best one we've heard all week.' They do not let Ozwyn pass.

mixed.text: The guards hesitate just long enough for Ozwyn to notice a side passage. Not a complete bluff, but the hesitation is useful.

success.text: Ozwyn delivers 'I'm the inspector' with such gravitas that four guards salute. One opens the gate. The sandwich guard shrugs and keeps eating.

crit.text: Ozwyn strides in, declares an 'official inspection,' fails every guard on cleanliness, writes up the sandwich guy, and walks out with a formal escort. GOAT liar.

crisis.title: The Final Trap
crisis.text: A corridor of swinging axes, floor spikes, dart walls, and one suspiciously placed banana peel. The dungeon's greatest hits, all in one hallway.

choices.label: Disarm the traps carefully
choices.hint: Patience and precision.

critFail.text: Ozwyn disarms the first trap and triggers the last three. The banana peel was the real threat. He knew it.

fail.text: Two traps disarmed, one activates. A dart grazes Ozwyn's ear. He'll never hear the same from that side.

mixed.text: Most traps click off. One dart gets through — caught it in his teeth. Not planned, but impressive.

success.text: Click. Click. Click. Every trap disarmed methodically. Ozwyn walks through the safe corridor feeling like a proper rogue.

crit.text: Ozwyn disarms every trap AND repurposes them to face the other direction. Any pursuing guards are in for a VERY bad day.

choices.label: Trigger everything and ride the chaos
choices.hint: Surfing on mayhem.

critFail.text: Ozwyn triggers everything at once. The traps fight EACH OTHER. Ozwyn is caught in the crossfire of an automated civil war.

fail.text: Chaos surfing requires more grace than Ozwyn has. He tumbles through, taking hits, and emerges looking like used confetti.

mixed.text: Ozwyn ducks, rolls, and accidentally dodges most traps. The banana peel still gets him. Always the banana peel.

success.text: Ozwyn SURFS an axe blade, DUCKS the darts, LEAPS the spikes. The banana peel? Kicked aside. He is SPEED.

crit.text: Ozwyn rides the chaos like a wave — bouncing off traps, spinning through axes, and using the banana peel as a sled to slide to safety. The dungeon itself applauds.

choices.label: Throw loot through to trigger traps
choices.hint: Sacrifice the gold to save the owlbear.

critFail.text: Ozwyn throws his entire bag and the traps destroy it. The bag, the loot, everything. He watches his treasure evaporate. Then a dart hits his knee.

fail.text: Not enough loot to trigger all traps. Ozwyn commits his supplies and still has to dodge the last section. Net loss.

mixed.text: The thrown loot triggers most traps. Ozwyn tiptoes through the remainder, mourning each lost coin like a fallen friend.

success.text: Strategic throwing. Minimum sacrifice, maximum trap activation. Ozwyn walks through safely with most of his loot. Efficient.

crit.text: Ozwyn throws one single coin. It ricochets off every trap trigger in sequence like a Rube Goldberg masterpiece. Every trap fires harmlessly. The coin rolls back to his feet. Genius.

crisis.title: Dungeon Warden
crisis.text: Warden Grimjaw blocks the final corridor, arms crossed. 'Ozwyn. Again.' She says it like a teacher catching a repeat offender.

choices.label: Talk your way out
choices.hint: Words are free. Freedom is priceless.

critFail.text: 'I can explain—' 'You stole the Duke's crown, crashed a wedding, and rode a horse through my office.' Ozwyn can NOT explain.

fail.text: Ozwyn's excuse is terrible. Grimjaw's eyebrow says everything. 'Back to your cell, Ozwyn.'

mixed.text: 'What if I return what I stole?' 'You don't even remember WHAT you stole.' Fair point. But Grimjaw sighs and looks tired. Negotiation window: open.

success.text: Ozwyn delivers a heartfelt apology, promises community service, and compliments Grimjaw's new haircut. She steps aside. 'This is the LAST time, Ozwyn.'

crit.text: Ozwyn monologues about destiny, freedom, and the nature of justice so beautifully that Grimjaw wipes a tear. 'Go. Before I change my mind.' Even wardens have hearts.

choices.label: Challenge her to a duel
choices.hint: Honor demands it.

critFail.text: Grimjaw disarms Ozwyn in one move and hangs him by his bag from a torch sconce. 'I trained for twenty years, Ozwyn. Twenty. Years.'

fail.text: Ozwyn lands one hit. Grimjaw lands twelve. It's not a duel, it's a lesson. Painful curriculum.

mixed.text: A surprisingly close fight. Grimjaw nods with grudging respect. 'You've improved since last time.' She still wins, but lets him pass. Pride tax.

success.text: Ozwyn fights with everything. Claws, beak, improvised weaponry. Grimjaw is pushed back for the first time in years. She salutes and steps aside.

crit.text: Ozwyn defeats Grimjaw in single combat. She kneels, surrenders her blade, and says, 'You've earned your freedom, owlbear.' On this day, a warrior was born.

choices.label: Pretend to be a different owlbear
choices.hint: All owlbears look the same, right? ...Right?

critFail.text: 'I'm... Bozwyn. Different owlbear entirely.' 'You have a name tag that says OZWYN.' Ozwyn looks down. He does indeed.

fail.text: 'You're wearing the same stolen pants as yesterday, Ozwyn.' Grimjaw's attention to detail is infuriating.

mixed.text: Grimjaw squints. 'You DO look different somehow.' The hat helps. She lets Ozwyn pass while radioing for confirmation. Time to MOVE.

success.text: 'I'm Ozwyn's cousin, Flozwyn. Here for a visit. Terrible dungeon, one star.' Grimjaw doesn't buy it but can't prove otherwise. Bureaucracy wins.

crit.text: 'I'm Inspector Thaddeus P. Featherbottom, Health and Safety.' Grimjaw pales. 'The inspection was TODAY?' She escorts Ozwyn out personally. GENIUS DISGUISE.

crisis.title: The Exit is Locked
crisis.text: Daylight. Sweet, beautiful daylight — just beyond a very locked door. Ozwyn can feel the outside air. He can taste freedom. The door disagrees.

choices.label: Pick the lock one last time
choices.hint: One more lock. The last lock. Come on.

critFail.text: The lockpick snaps. The backup snaps. Ozwyn punches the lock and hurts his paw. SO CLOSE. So painfully close.

fail.text: This lock is military-grade. Ozwyn's pick skills aren't enough. He can hear birds outside. Mocking birds.

mixed.text: The lock gives slowly. It takes ten tense minutes. Ozwyn hears guards approaching. The lock clicks open as footsteps echo. GO GO GO.

success.text: Click. The door swings open. Fresh air hits Ozwyn's face. He almost cries. The lock whispers 'good luck.' Even dungeon hardware roots for him.

crit.text: Ozwyn picks the lock so fast it sets a dungeon record. He steps into sunlight, free and victorious. Behind him, the lock starts a slow clap.

choices.label: Find another way out
choices.hint: Doors are suggestions.

critFail.text: Ozwyn finds a window. It's barred. He finds a chimney. It's too small. He finds a doggy door. It's a trap. The dungeon hates him.

fail.text: The alternative route exists but it's a sewer grate. Ozwyn squeezes through and emerges smelling like a swamp's armpit.

mixed.text: A side passage leads to a low window. Ozwyn barely squeezes through, losing some loot in the process but gaining freedom.

success.text: A forgotten service tunnel exits behind the dungeon. Ozwyn emerges into a garden. He takes a flower as a souvenir and walks away.

crit.text: Ozwyn discovers the dungeon architect's personal escape route — pristine, unguarded, with mood lighting. He steps into freedom like a prince exiting a palace.

choices.label: Kick the door down
choices.hint: Subtlety had its chance.

critFail.text: Ozwyn kicks the reinforced door. The door wins. Ozwyn bounces back, holding his foot, hopping in pain. The guards find him hopping.

fail.text: WHAM. The door shudders but holds. Again. WHAM. Holds. Ozwyn's foot disagrees with this strategy.

mixed.text: Three kicks and the door cracks. Not open, but cracked. One more should do it — if Ozwyn's foot survives.

success.text: BOOM. One mighty owlbear kick. The door EXPLODES off its hinges into the courtyard. Guards scatter. Ozwyn stands in the doorframe, backlit by daylight. ICONIC.

crit.text: Ozwyn kicks the door into LOW ORBIT. It rockets across the courtyard, over the wall, and into the river. The dungeon is now open-air. Freedom was always one kick away.

endings.title: The Owlbear Who Stole Everything
endings.text: Ozwyn walks into the sunset carrying more treasure than he entered with. The dungeon was supposed to be a punishment. It was a shopping trip.
endings.shareQuote: I went into a dungeon as a prisoner and left with more loot than the dungeon had. Productive trip.

endings.title: Dungeon Breaker
endings.text: They'll have to rebuild the dungeon after Ozwyn's escape. Walls, doors, morale — all shattered. He didn't escape captivity. He defeated it.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped a dungeon and destroyed it in the process. Property damage included at no extra charge.

endings.title: The Legend of the Headache
endings.text: Ozwyn escaped a dungeon while nursing the worst headache of his life. Bards will sing of this. Badly, but loudly.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped a dungeon with a splitting headache. Bards are singing about it. Loudly and badly.

endings.title: Freedom's Favorite Fool
endings.text: Nothing Ozwyn did made sense. Every choice was wrong. And yet — here he stands, free, rich, and grinning. Some owlbears are just blessed by chaos.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped captivity, freed six others, and started a revolution. I was only in there for stealing a pie.

endings.title: The One Who Walked Out
endings.text: Ozwyn didn't run. Didn't sneak. He walked out the front gate on a streak of unstoppable momentum. Coolest owlbear alive.
endings.shareQuote: I walked out the front door. No plan. No backup. Just absolute confidence. They let me go.

endings.title: The Reluctant Hero
endings.text: Ozwyn didn't want to be a hero. He just wanted to leave. But along the way, he helped everyone he met, because that's who he is. Damn it.
endings.shareQuote: I didn't want to be anyone's hero. I just wanted to leave. Turns out those are the same thing.

endings.title: The Bleeding Champion
endings.text: Battered, bruised, and still going. Ozwyn escaped not because he was strong, but because he refused to stop. Blood and stubbornness — the owlbear way.
endings.shareQuote: I bled through two acts, kept moving, and somehow became a symbol of hope. Bandages included.

endings.title: Honor Among Owlbears
endings.text: Ozwyn left the dungeon with less than he found — because he gave it away. The treasure was never the point. Freedom was. And maybe the friends along the way.
endings.shareQuote: I made friends with an owlbear in a dungeon. We share custody of a stolen gem now.

endings.title: Guardian of the Rat
endings.text: Ozwyn escaped with a loyal rat on his shoulder and the respect of every creature in the dungeon. Some heroes wear capes. Ozwyn wears a rodent.
endings.shareQuote: I protected a rat the entire time. Nobody asked me to. He didn't even say thank you.

endings.title: Ozwyn the Unbreakable
endings.text: No supplies. No plan. Nothing but grit and feathers. Ozwyn escaped with almost nothing and earned everything. The hardest road made the strongest owlbear.
endings.shareQuote: They called me unbreakable. They were mostly right. My left knee disagrees.

endings.title: The Beloved Disaster
endings.text: Everything Ozwyn touched went sideways, but everyone he met liked him anyway. Charisma is nature's apology for chaos.
endings.shareQuote: I accidentally collapsed a wall, flooded a corridor, and became a folk hero. Not intentional.

endings.title: Accidentally in Charge
endings.text: Through a series of escalating misunderstandings, Ozwyn is now believed to be the dungeon's new warden. He doesn't correct them. Free room and board, baby.
endings.shareQuote: I somehow became the dungeon's acting warden. I have no memory of agreeing to this.

endings.title: Dungeon Interior Designer
endings.text: Ozwyn didn't just escape — he rearranged the dungeon on his way out. New trap placements, better lighting, motivational graffiti. The Warden finds a Yelp review pinned to her door.
endings.shareQuote: I rearranged the dungeon and left. The guards are still finding things I moved.

endings.title: The Owlbear Express
endings.text: Ozwyn didn't walk out. He BARRELLED out — through walls, guards, and basic physics. The dungeon has an owlbear-shaped hole now. It's a tourist attraction.
endings.shareQuote: I rode an owlbear out of a dungeon at full speed. I did not plan this. I do not regret it.

endings.title: Professional Improviser
endings.text: Ozwyn had no plan. He never had a plan. The plan was 'no plan.' And against all logic, it worked. Again.
endings.shareQuote: My escape plan was improvised at every step. It worked. The dungeon did not see that coming.

endings.title: The Glittering Ruin
endings.text: Ozwyn escaped rich and ruined. His pockets overflow with treasure but every piece is cursed. He sparkles in the moonlight like a very sad disco ball.
endings.shareQuote: I found beautiful cursed treasure and took all of it. My future looks shiny and terrible.

endings.title: Headache Level: Mythic
endings.text: Ozwyn's headache has evolved into a permanent companion. It has opinions. It has a name. Gerald the Headache disapproves of everything.
endings.shareQuote: The headache I left with is now considered a legendary affliction. Healers are baffled.

endings.title: Permanently Lost
endings.text: Ozwyn is outside. Technically. He's also in a swamp he doesn't recognize, three kingdoms from home, with nothing but vibes and confusion.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped by getting so lost I accidentally found the exit. Twice.

endings.title: The Warden's New Assistant
endings.text: Grimjaw was so impressed by Ozwyn's escape attempt she offered him a job. Ozwyn now works at the dungeon. On purpose. The pay is decent.
endings.shareQuote: The warden hired me on the way out. I haven't figured out how to quit yet.

endings.title: Haunted by the Lock
endings.text: Ozwyn escaped but the talking lock followed him home. It criticizes his cooking, judges his friends, and won't stop asking for passwords. Freedom has a price.
endings.shareQuote: A lock cursed me on the way out. Every door I touch whispers now. I have not slept well since.

endings.title: Local Fool, Regional Icon
endings.text: Ozwyn is famous now. Not for being good at escaping — for being hilariously bad at it. Tavern bards can't stop writing ballads. He gets free drinks out of pity.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped a dungeon and became a regional icon for all the wrong reasons. Don't ask.

endings.title: Nap Champion
endings.text: Ozwyn ran out of supplies, ran out of energy, and gave up in a sunny patch outside the dungeon. He's free, technically. He's also asleep. Classic Ozwyn.
endings.shareQuote: I fell asleep in a dungeon cell and nobody noticed me leave. Most peaceful escape on record.

endings.title: The Loot Was Fake
endings.text: All of it. Every coin, every gem, every 'treasure' — theatrical props from the dungeon drama club. Ozwyn escaped with a bag of painted rocks and broken dreams.
endings.shareQuote: Every treasure I collected was fake. I carried it for six rooms before someone told me.

endings.title: Emotional Damage Only
endings.text: Ozwyn is physically fine. Emotionally? Devastated. The dungeon broke nothing but his spirit, his confidence, and his faith in locks. He needs a hug.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped physically unharmed but emotionally shattered. The dungeon knew exactly what it was doing.

endings.title: Not Again...
endings.text: Ozwyn stumbles out, blinks at the sun, and realizes he's been here before. This is the fourth dungeon this month. He really needs to stop drinking at that tavern.
endings.shareQuote: I escaped only to discover I was in the wrong dungeon the whole time. Still counts.
